title: Archer Aviation Shares Slip As Heavy Cash Burn Overshadows Q1 Revenue Beat
text: Archer Aviation shares experienced trading volatility in May 2026 as the market digested strong Q1 results alongside concerns about the company's cash burn rate. The stock has shown significant volatility with a beta of 3.13, posting a 13% gain over the past week while down 27% over six months. Archer delivered robust performance on May 6, 2026, with shares surging 9.76%, and the stock's momentum was underscored by exceptional trading volume as the company recorded a trading turnover of $0.34 billion, ranking first in daily trading activity across the broader market. Archer Aviation shares are trading lower as risk appetite is fading across high-beta names, even after the company's recent first-quarter results highlighted progress on certification and 2026 operating plans. The stock can offer sharp moves both ways around news and guidance, and that volatility is what many traders hunt. The tension between regulatory progress and cash burn constraints often creates volatility. The stock is characterized as highly reactive to news catalysts and regulatory updates regarding its eVTOL aircraft certification progress.
